behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because this is parser/orchestration across git-output parsing and report aggregation. PASS2-AUDIT: The failure boundary is real git rename detection semantics (similarity thresholds, status encoding) plus CLI execution against an actual repo; mocked git output can miss real rename edge cases and always pass. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE => needs real git subprocess + real repo state, so System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git diff categorization; no AI/ML call and no multi-agent pipeline, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp real git repo, commit baseline, perform `git mv` (and content edits to exercise rename detection), run the actual CLI binary, and assert report has the file only in `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because binary-diff handling often depends on actual git subprocess output and file contents on disk. PASS2-AUDIT: Could Integration with canned diff text catch some parser bugs, but crash behavior here is specifically tied to real binary detection/encoding behavior from git and real files; mocks can hide decoding/subprocess-path failures. Keep System. Rejected higher levels: behavior concerns binary file handling in git diffs only; no AI/ML output dependency or multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify at least one binary file (e.g., random bytes), invoke CLI, assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file in counts/category without traceback.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since it could be treated as handling a specific git error/path. PASS2-AUDIT: The real failure appears when CLI hits actual git behavior for unborn HEAD in a real repository; faked responses may not match exact stderr/exit-code combinations. REAL-INFRA pushes to System. Rejected higher levels: no model decision-making or agent pipeline in this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run CLI, assert zero exit code and structured empty report (all categories/counts empty/zero) rather than an exception/error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because correctness is explicitly against the actual repository history, not a synthetic fixture. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration tests with mocked git data only validate counting logic, but can miss real-history traversal issues (rev ranges, rename treatment, merge/history interpretation) that arise only with real git + repo state. Keep System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is deterministic VCS accounting with no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known sequence of add/delete/rename/modify commits, run CLI over the target range/default window, and assert each reported category count exactly equals the known ground truth from the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because staged/unstaged equivalence depends on real git index vs working tree semantics. PASS2-AUDIT: In-process stubs cannot faithfully reproduce index/worktree edge cases (renames, mode bits, partial staging), so MOCK-HIDE applies; real git repo + subprocess required. Keep System. Rejected higher levels: this behavior is about git state comparison only; no AI model output and no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets in two scenarios (A: staged, B: unstaged), run CLI in both states, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout report.
